mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets compress wage growth and signal peak cyclical demand, causing equity risk premiums to expand and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the low unemployment signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and contraction in real retail sales indicates supply-side bottlenecks outpacing consumer demand, triggering margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 50% of historical instances where INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth by >1 standard deviation over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, an increase in the federal funds rate combined with declining housing starts signals aggressive monetary tightening that crushes real estate valuations and broader equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in >40% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and HOUST move in the specified directions, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate earnings fundamentals, which directly supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.20, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 2.5% annualized growth, persistent inflation erodes real yields and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity risk appetite and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >55% of instances following CPI readings above the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth coupled with accelerating real retail sales reflects a self-reinforcing demand boom that initially lifts corporate profits but subsequently triggers overheating fears and valuation mean-reversion, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts positive returns in >50% of historical episodes where both PAYEMS and RSAFS show month-over-month growth exceeding their 10-year averages, or the interaction coefficient in a factor model is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial capacity, a surge in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of infrastructure and construction demand, boosting cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in >45% of cases where INDPRO is below 105 and HOUST rises sequentially, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.
